index.hero_eyebrow: // ALL IN IS THE ONLY WAY OUT
index.hero_sub: No Half Measures exists to inspire wholehearted living and transform broken lives through community, education, opportunity, and accountability.
index.mission_subhead: We meet people where they are and ask, "how can I help?"
index.pillar1_body: We connect them to resources and model lives that show recovery is possible.
index.pillar2_body: We provide job training and opportunities that expand their vision.
index.pillar3_body: Housing — a stable place to land during critical seasons of rebuilding.
index.pillar4_body: Community to lean on when life gets heavy and hope feels out of reach.
index.ground_subhead: We don't help from a distance. Real people. Real rooms. Real recoveries.
index.ask_sub: Your gift funds recovery — housing, training, and the brotherhood that keeps men sober. Every dollar goes to the work.

donate.hero_sub: Every dollar funds recovery — job training, housing, community. No overhead games. No half measures. Your gift goes straight to the work.
donate.choose_lead: Pick an amount — or enter your own. Then give in seconds through PayPal or Venmo. Every gift is tax-deductible.
donate.note_heading: No overhead games. No half measures.
donate.note_body: You'll receive a confirmation and a tax receipt at the email tied to your payment. Every dollar goes to the ground — housing, training, and the brotherhood that keeps men sober long after the program ends.

recap.hero_sub: This night marked the beginning of something that doesn't go halfway. Below you'll find Don's full talk and photos from the night.
recap.pull_quote: "All in is the only way out."
recap.recap_p1: On May 23rd, you packed the Silk Mill and helped us launch something that matters. We worshiped together, we heard Don share how he got from the bottom back to life, and we got our first real look at what No Half Measures is built to do: walk with people through addiction, through recovery, and through everything that comes after.
recap.recap_p2: None of this works from a distance. It works because people show up, look each other in the eye, and refuse to let go. That is exactly what you did on the 23rd. That is the whole mission in one room.
recap.recap_p3: Below you'll find the photos from the night and Don's full talk. If it moved you, there is a way to stay in it.
recap.newsletter_sub: Join our mailing list to receive updates and the Hope Merchant Newsletter, launching soon.
